mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth quartiles do not monotonically rank S&P 500 20-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the federal funds rate of more than 50 basis points over a trailing 3-month window signals an active monetary tightening cycle that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations where FEDFUNDS rises more than 50 basis points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low unemployment below 4.5 percent and consumer price inflation above 2.0 percent force the central bank to accelerate monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of UNRATE below 4.5 percent and CPIAUCSL year-over-year above 2.0 percent fail to produce negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5 percentage points over six months, starting from a level above 5 percent, signals early-cycle labor market healing that boosts consumer spending confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5pp over 6 months entering from above 5%
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE falls more than 0.5 percentage points from above 5 percent, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive at the 5 percent level in a t-test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening household demand that directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS declines are not followed by negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in at least 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales confirm a broad contraction across supply and demand, amplifying recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM and RSAFS declines MoM
failure_condition: Concurrent month-over-month declines in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to produce negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent signals an overheating price environment that pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL year-over-year above 2.5 percent does not coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2 percent annualized signals durable aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2 percent annualized does not produce positive average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns over a rolling 3-year window.
